title: passerina nitida
side_effects: Passerina nitida can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, skin irritation, and allergic reactions. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while topical application may result in skin rashes or burning sensations. Some individuals may also experience respiratory problems, such as coughing or shortness of breath, due to the plant's potential release of volatile compounds. Improper use of Passerina nitida may also interact with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
